initiative.name: OpenIncident v0.4 - On-Call Scheduling & Rotations
initiative.description: Introduce on-call scheduling as a first-class feature. Users can define schedules with layer-based rotations (daily, weekly, custom), create overrides, query who is currently on call, and receive Slack notifications when shifts change. This is the foundation for escalation policies in v0.5.
success_criteria: User can create a schedule with one or more rotation layers (daily or weekly)
GET /api/v1/schedules/:id/oncall returns the correct on-call user for the current time
Schedule overrides allow one-off coverage changes without modifying the base rotation
Calendar UI renders the current rotation visually with upcoming shifts
Slack notifications posted when a shift handoff occurs
initiative.architecture_notes: Schedule evaluation is time-based: given a timestamp, walk rotation layers in order-index priority to find the on-call user. Overrides shadow the base rotation for their time window. A background worker (or cron job) fires Slack shift notifications at handoff times. No Redis dependency for schedule evaluation — pure in-memory computation from DB records.
